events.event_description: The American Legation in St. Petersburg was established when U.S. Minister John Quincy Adams presented his credentials to Emperor Alexander I.

evidence.quote: The American Legation in St. Petersburg was established on November 5, 1809, when U.S. Minister to Russia John Quincy Adams presented his credentials to Emperor Alexander I.

events.event_description: The American Legation in St. Petersburg was raised to an Embassy when Ethan A. Hitchcock was appointed Ambassador to Russia.

evidence.quote: The American Legation in St. Petersburg was raised to an Embassy on February 11, 1898, when Ethan A. Hitchcock was appointed Ambassador to Russia.

events.event_description: The American Embassy at Archangel was closed, ending formal U.S. diplomatic presence in Russia.

evidence.quote: The American Embassy at Archangel was closed on September 14, 1919.

events.event_description: The American Embassy was established in Moscow when William Bullitt presented his credentials to President Kalinin as U.S. Ambassador to the Soviet Union, resuming formal diplomatic representation after a 14-year break.

evidence.quote: On December 13, 1933, William Bullitt presented his credentials to President Kalinin in Moscow as the U.S. Ambassador to the Soviet Union.
